title: What This Fund's $24 Million monday.com Sale Could Signal After a 73% Stock Drop
author: Jonathan Ponciano
description: ShawSpring Partners exited its entire 253,959-share position in monday.com for approximately $24.37 million as the stock has declined 73% over the past year. Despite the sharp stock decline, monday.com's fundamentals remain strong with 24% revenue growth, doubled operating income, and expanding enterprise adoption, suggesting the market may be overly pessimistic about the company's prospects.

insights.sentiment_reasoning: While the stock has suffered a severe 73% decline, the underlying business fundamentals are strong with 24% YoY revenue growth, doubled operating income, 39-74% growth in high-value customers, and $880M in remaining performance obligations. The disconnect between weak stock performance and strong operational metrics suggests the market may be overly pessimistic, warranting a neutral stance that acknowledges both the risks and potential value opportunity.

Mentioned as a top holding of ShawSpring Partners ($38.84M, 14.5% of AUM) with no specific news or performance commentary in the article. Neutral sentiment reflects its role as a portfolio holding without additional context.
Listed as ShawSpring's largest holding ($44.47M, 16.6% of AUM) with no specific news or performance information provided in the article.

title: What to Know About a $38 Million Bet on monday.com Amid a 75% Stock Drop
author: Jonathan Ponciano
description: Conifer Management acquired 400,000 shares of monday.com for $38.39 million in Q1 2026, despite the stock falling 75% over the past year. The investment appears contrarian, as the company continues to show strong fundamentals with 24% YoY revenue growth, profitability, $100M+ quarterly operating cash flow, and expanding high-value customer segments. monday.com recently launched an AI Work Platform and raised full-year revenue guidance to $1.47 billion.

insights.sentiment_reasoning: Despite severe stock underperformance (-75% YoY), the company demonstrates strong operational metrics: 24% revenue growth, doubled operating income, $100M+ operating cash flow, 39-74% growth in high-value customers, and new AI platform launch with raised guidance. The disconnect between stock price and business health suggests undervaluation.

title: Monday.com Stock Analysis: Buy or Sell?
author: Parkev Tatevosian, Cfa
description: The Motley Fool analyzes Monday.com (MNDY) as a potentially overlooked stock worth considering. The article examines the company's recent earnings performance and stock movement, suggesting it may present an interesting investment opportunity.

insights.sentiment_reasoning: The article describes Monday.com as 'one of the most overlooked stocks on the market' and frames it as 'an interesting stock to consider,' suggesting potential upside. The Motley Fool has positions in and recommends the stock, indicating confidence in its prospects.

title: What Should Investors Do With the New Wave of IPOs?
author: Motley Fool Staff
description: The Motley Fool discusses upcoming IPOs including Cerebras, an AI semiconductor company valued at $50 billion. The team analyzes Monday.com's strong earnings beat and Cerebras's potential as a Nvidia alternative, while advising investors to deploy fresh capital rather than trim winners to chase IPO hype. They recommend waiting for initial volatility to settle before investing in new IPOs.

insights.sentiment_reasoning: Company beat analyst expectations on revenue ($351M, 24% YoY growth) and earnings ($1.15 EPS vs $0.93 expected), raised 2026 guidance, showed strong enterprise expansion (39% growth in $100K+ ARR customers), and doubled operating margins to 6%. However, growth is decelerating from 25% to expected 18-19%, raising questions about market maturity.

Strong bull case with unique wafer-scale architecture, 21x faster inference than Nvidia GPUs, major deals with AWS and OpenAI, and 76% YoY revenue growth. However, significant bear cases include extreme customer concentration (86% from two UAE entities in 2025), $50B valuation at 100x sales with no margin for error, Nvidia's competing products, and lack of GAAP profitability. Analysts recommend waiting for hype to settle before buying.

Mentioned as the dominant incumbent in AI chips with unlimited R&D budget and competing products in development against Cerebras. While positioned as a competitive threat to challengers, no direct analysis of Nvidia's fundamentals provided in this article.
Mentioned as a hyperscaler that signed a binding term sheet with Cerebras in March to deploy their systems in AWS data centers, indicating strategic partnership in AI infrastructure.

title: Why Monday.com Stock Crushed it Today
author: Eric Volkman
description: Monday.com stock surged nearly 7% after delivering a double beat on analyst expectations in Q1 2026. The company reported revenue of $351 million (24% YoY growth) and non-GAAP net income of $1.17 per share, exceeding consensus estimates of $339 million and $0.95 per share respectively. The company attributed gains to consumption-based pricing and recently launched an AI-powered Work Platform. Full-year 2026 revenue guidance of $1.47 billion also topped analyst consensus.

insights.sentiment_reasoning: Strong Q1 earnings results with 24% revenue growth, double beat on analyst estimates for both revenue and EPS, positive full-year guidance exceeding consensus, and launch of new AI-powered platform positioning the company for future growth. Stock price reflected this with a 6.86% gain.
